generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components (Physical Acting Components)
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor in the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot generating torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω) outputs. Operates within restricted working range [-90°, 90°] with max angular velocity |q̇_max| = 75°/s."
]

instance GearWheelComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component in the first axis transmission system of MABI MAX 100 robot, transmitting torque (τ_G) between motor and cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance CycloidalGearboxComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox in the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot providing high torque transmission with precision gearing for manufacturing applications."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoderSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular displacement data for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoderSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing real-time output angle data for backlash and positional uncertainty analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller measuring input current (I_M) to the motor for torque derivation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoderSensor
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoderSensor
]

instance CurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the structural composition of the first axis (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their rotational/current flow definitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction for comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model for joint friction analysis in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model accounting for stiction effects in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model for dynamic friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model for detailed friction behavior analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-provided dynamics model for torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) calculations from current measurements."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCalculationEnabler
]

instance GearboxDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-provided dynamics model for torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) calculations through gear transmission."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCalculationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredInputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time time-series data of input current (I_M) to the motor streamed via MQTT protocol from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCalculationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time time-series data of output angle from secondary encoder streamed via MQTT protocol for positional analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCalculationEnabler
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque (τ̂_M) data calculated from measured input current using manufacturer-provided motor dynamics model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredInputCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DerivedMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived angular velocity (ω̂_M) data calculated from encoder measurements using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque (τ̂_G) data from friction models representing gearbox internal friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated output torque (τ̂_out) data incorporating all friction models for comparison with physical measurements."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LuGreFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataAcquisitionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, encoder outputs) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService
]

instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML tooling for creating and maintaining structural models (BDDs/IBDs) of the first axis components and their relationships."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment executing friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and dynamics simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance DataCalculationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic processing for deriving torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) from measured inputs using manufacturer-specific dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataDerivationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard for integrated visualization of measured vs. simulated data (torque, angular velocity deviations) with friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell implementation providing semantic interoperability for digital shadow components and data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteroperabilityService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time data acquisition service streaming time-series data (I_M, encoder outputs) from physical system to digital shadow via MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAcquisitionInsight
]

instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for maintaining and updating SysML structural models (BDDs/IBDs) representing the physical components of the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralModelInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comprehensive friction analysis service combining white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models to characterize joint friction in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelRefinementAction
]

instance DataDerivationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for deriving physical quantities (torque, angular velocity) from raw sensor data using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:provides DerivedDataInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing unified visualization of measured vs. simulated data to identify friction-induced deviations in motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyInsight
]

instance InteroperabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring semantic interoperability between digital shadow components through standardized Asset Administration Shells."
    DTDFVocab:provides TraceabilityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataAcquisitionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time data acquisition status and quality metrics for input current (I_M) and output angle measurements from physical system."
]

instance StructuralModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current state of SysML structural models representing the first axis components and their relationships for validation purposes."
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the cycloidal gearbox based on operational data."
]

instance DerivedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quality metrics and validation status for derived torque (τ̂) and angular velocity (ω̂) data from sensor measurements."
]

instance MotionAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantified friction-induced inaccuracies in robot motion (up to 30% of total error) with recommendations for control algorithm corrections."
]

instance TraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Complete traceability of data origins and processing steps from physical sensors through simulation models to final insights."
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated parameter updates for friction models based on operational data deviations to account for wear, temperature, and load variations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DiagnosticAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated anomaly detection for sudden changes in friction behavior (e.g., wear) triggering maintenance planning alerts."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance RobotAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot including temperature, load, and manufacturing process conditions."
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearWheelComponent, CycloidalGearboxComponent,
                 MotorEncoderSensor, SecondaryEncoderSensor, CurrentSensor
]

// C13: Component
instance FirstAxisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot comprising motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox with restricted working range [-90°, 90°]."
    base:isContainedIn RobotAxisEnvironment
]

instance DigitalShadowComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Purpose-specific digital shadow focusing on joint friction analysis for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
    base:isContainedIn RobotAxisEnvironment
]

// Added missing SystemUnderStudy instance
instance MABIMAX100System : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "MABI MAX 100 industrial robot system with six axes focusing on the first vertical axis for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment RobotAxisEnvironment
    base:contains FirstAxisComponent, DigitalShadowComponent
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronizing with the physical system’s operational frequency. Data is processed at **high temporal resolution** (millisecond-level granularity) to enable real-time friction identification. The digital shadow updates dynamically with **live sensor data streams** (e.g., MQTT) and **simulation models** (e.g., Simulink), ensuring low-latency analysis for motion accuracy improvements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is structured as follows:
  - **Core Components**:
    - **Digital Shadow (FrictionShadow)**: Purpose-specific abstraction for friction analysis, incorporating **Purpose (FrictionDerivation)**, **Asset (Mabi)**, and **Processing components** (Simulation M, GW, GB).
    - **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders) and derived data (e.g., torque from dynamics models).
    - **Models**: Linked engineering (SysML), simulation (Simulink), and data calculation models.
  - **Data Flow**:
    1. Physical system → **MQTT streams** → Digital Shadow (data ingestion).
    2. Digital Shadow → **Processing components** (Simulink) → Simulated friction outputs.
    3. Unified dashboard → **Visualization** of deviations between measured and simulated data.
  - **Orchestration**: Centralized pipeline with **traceable data provenance**, enabling end-to-end analysis of friction effects on motion accuracy."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates during the **operational phase** of the physical system’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representations of the first axis. It does not model the **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** phases but instead **continuously refines its models** based on real-time operational data to improve motion accuracy. The digital shadow is **dynamic and adaptive**, evolving alongside the physical system’s wear and usage patterns."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Comparison of **measured vs. simulated data** (e.g., torque, angular velocity) to validate friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, etc.).
  - **Uncertainty Quantification**: Analysis of deviations between **reference data (measured)** and **simulated outputs** (e.g., ˆτ_M, ˆω_G) to assess model accuracy.
  - **Parameter Identification**: Continuous **adjustment of friction model parameters** during operation to account for **wear, temperature, and load variations**.
  - **Verification Mechanisms**: **Traceability** of data origins (e.g., MQTT channels) and processing steps (Simulink models) to ensure reproducibility.
  - **Error Sources**: Addressed through **dual encoder systems** (motor + secondary encoder) to mitigate backlash and positional uncertainty."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is established via:
  - **Protocol**: **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for **real-time, low-latency data streaming** of sensor data (I_M, encoder outputs).
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data with **floating-point values** for torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω), structured as **DataTraces** in the digital shadow.
  - **Network Architecture**: **Unified dashboard** aggregates and visualizes data from the digital shadow, enabling **cross-model correlation** (e.g., SysML + Simulink). No explicit mention of cloud/edge deployment is provided; the system appears **locally integrated** with the CNC controller."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is **locally deployed** alongside the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller, leveraging **on-premise infrastructure** for real-time processing. The **dashboard visualization** is likely hosted on the same local network or integrated into the CNC system’s interface. No cloud-based deployment or distributed hosting is mentioned; the system relies on **local computation resources** (e.g., MATLAB Simulink runtime) for processing."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards:
  - **Modeling**: SysML (OMG standard) for **engineering models** (BDDs, IBDs).
  - **Asset Representation**: **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (IEC 62424) for semantic interoperability and data traceability.
  - **Simulation**: MATLAB Simulink (industry-standard tool) for **executable behavioral models**.
  - **Data Protocols**: MQTT (IETF standard) for **real-time data streaming**.
  The methodology also references **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** for structured digital shadow construction."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s security and safety considerations include:
  - **No Remote Actuation**: The digital shadow is **passive**; it does not execute operations remotely on the physical system, mitigating risks of unintended control interventions.
  - **Data Isolation**: Sensor data (e.g., MQTT streams) is **locally processed** without explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication). The system relies on the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl.** controller’s inherent security for data integrity.
  - **Safety Constraints**: The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°], ensuring operational limits are respected. However, **no explicit safety protocols** (e.g., fail-safes, emergency stops) are linked to the digital twin’s outputs.
  - **Human Safety**: The digital twin’s purpose is **analytical** (friction modeling), with no direct interaction with human operators during operation."
]
